assertions.description: The analyzer correctly counts all component types. Verified by cross-referencing source: 40 resistors (R1-R40 minus a few), 50 capacitors (C1-C48 plus CT1, CT2), 14 ICs (U1-U14), 5 inductors (L1-L5), 1 crystal (Y1), 4 connectors (J1, P1, P2, P3), 1 LED (D1), 2 diodes (D2, D3), 3 test points (TP1, TP2, TP3), 3 fiducials (FID1-FID3), 5 other (W1-W5). Totals match the source.

Spot-checking key BOM entries: R2=1100k 1%, R3=910k 1%, R5=36k 1%, R6=3k 1%, R13=3.3k 1%, R16=13.3k 1%, R14=22k 1%, R15=5.6k 1%, R4=1.96M 1%, R24=470 1%, R30=47 (no unit), C3=47n 100V, C5=10p, C6=220p — all match the source exactly.

All 14 unique ICs are correctly identified: U1 (LPC4370FET100, TFBGA100), U2/U4 (CPC1017N, solid-state relay), U3 (AMS1117-3.3 LDO from AP1117D33 symbol), U5 (OPA4354AIPWR, quad op-amp), U6 (LM2664, charge pump), U7 (74HC4052PW, dual 4-to-1 analog mux), U8 (W25Q80BVSSIG, SPI flash), U9 (TC7W53FK), U10 (USBLC6-2SC6, USB ESD), U11/U12 (LM385M3-1.2/NOPB, voltage reference), U13 (AT24C02C-XHM-T, I2C EEPROM), U14 (MCP4726A0T-E/CH, I2C DAC).

The schematic uses a custom analog supply pair (ALT_VAA+ and ALT_VAA-) generated by the LM2664 charge pump (U6) and a 3.3V digital rail (ALT_3.3VD) from the AMS1117-3.3 LDO (U3) powered from VBUS. The analyzer correctly reports all 6 power rails including the rescued GND symbol variant.

The source instantiates U5 five times: unit 1 (P 4550 2200), unit 2 (P 15450 2850), unit 3 (P 12250 2750), unit 4 (P 4550 4050), unit 5 (P 5450 1300). The analyzer correctly reports all 5 units in opamp_circuits. The OPA4354 is indeed a quad op-amp with a separate power-supply unit, so 5 units is correct.

The source splits U1 (LPC4370FET100) into units 1-4 placed at various sheet regions (P 1600 14000, P 12900 7100, P 18350 12850, P 8950 11300). The analyzer creates 4 separate subcircuit entries for U1, matching the 4 units in the source. The BOM correctly collapses them into 1 quantity.

Y1 is correctly placed in crystal_circuits. However, frequency is null even though the part value string 'ABLS-12.000MHZ-B4-T' encodes the frequency, and load_caps is empty even though C36 (18p) and C38 (18p) are connected directly to Y1 pins in the schematic.

The analyzer reports 51 total nets, which matches the rich set of global labels in the design: GND, ALT_3.3VD, ALT_VAA+, ALT_VAA-, VBUS, I2C_SDA, I2C_SCL, ADCHS_VPOS0, ADCHS_VNEG, DAC_VREF, DAC_OUT, BIAS, MUX0-S0, MUX0-S1, MUX1-A, JTAG_TMS/TCK/TDI/TDO/RESET, SPIFI_CS/SCK/MOSI/MISO/SIO2/SIO3, BOOT0-3, BOOT_ISP, DC_SW, OP_SW, USB_D+, USB_D-, USB_VBUS, LED0, PROBE_COMP, EEPROM_WP, plus several unnamed nets. All are correctly captured.
The analyzer correctly detects USB_D+ and USB_D- as a differential USB pair in design_analysis.differential_pairs.
All 5 JTAG signals are present in the nets and labels sections. P3 is a 7-pin 1.27mm pitch JTAG header. The JTAG signals are classified as 'debug' in net_classification, which is appropriate.
The analyzer reports total_no_connects: 56, matching the extensive NoConn markers placed on unused U1 (LPC4370) pins throughout the schematic. The no_connects array is populated with coordinates.

CT1 (TZC3R100A110, 3p-10p Murata trimmer) and CT2 (TZC3Z030A110R00, 1.4p-3p trimmer) are placed in the BOM as type 'capacitor' with the full value strings as given in the source, which is appropriate for trimmer/variable capacitors.

In the source, C36 (18p, at P 14650 7950) and C38 (18p, at P 15150 7950) are connected to the two pins of Y1 (at P 14900 7650). Both go to GND-RESCUE via #PWR034. The crystal_circuits entry reports load_caps: [] instead of [C36, C38]. The load capacitance is 18pF per pin, consistent with the 12 MHz crystal spec.

The schematic shows two precision attenuator chains from J1 (BNC input). The low-range channel uses R2 (1100k), R3 (910k), CT1 (3p-10p trimmer), and C5 (10p) to form a frequency-compensated 40% divider. The high-range channel uses R4 (1.96M), R5 (36k), CT2 (1.4p-3p trimmer), and C6 (220p) for a 1.8% attenuator. These are classic probe-style compensation dividers. voltage_dividers: [] in the output.

Multiple RC filter pairs surround U7 (74HC4052PW mux) and the op-amp inputs. For example: R13 (3.3k) + C28 (15p) on the channel 1 path, R14 (22k) + C29 (3.3p), R16 (13.3k) + C32 (4.7p), R15 (5.6k) + C31 (10p). These form anti-aliasing filters for the ADC front end. rc_filters: [] in the output.

U5 units 1-4 are op-amp signal conditioning stages. All 5 units (including the power unit) are reported as configuration 'unknown'. The neighbor_components lists are empty for all subcircuit entries. For a design this complex (oscilloscope front-end), the analyzer is providing no useful topology information about the op-amp stages despite extensive passive component context.

The schematic has a clear I2C bus: nets I2C_SDA and I2C_SCL connect U13 (AT24C02C-XHM-T, I2C EEPROM) and U14 (MCP4726A0T-E/CH, I2C DAC) to U1 (LPC4370, master). Pull-up resistors R20 (4.7k) and R22 (4.7k) connect SDA/SCL to ALT_3.3VD. Test points TP1 (SCL) and TP2 (SDA) are on the bus. bus_analysis.i2c: [] in the output, and I2C_SDA/I2C_SCL nets have empty pins lists.

Nets SPIFI_CS, SPIFI_SCK, SPIFI_MOSI, SPIFI_MISO, SPIFI_SIO2, SPIFI_SIO3 connect U1 (LPC4370) to U8 (W25Q80BVSSIG SPI flash). This is clearly a SPIFI (SPI Flash Interface) bus. bus_analysis.spi: [] in the output.

U6 (LM2664) is a switched-capacitor voltage inverter (charge pump) that takes ALT_VAA+ and generates ALT_VAA-. It is accompanied by C4, C18, C25, C26, C27, L2, L4 on the supply rails. This is the negative rail generator for the analog front-end. The power_regulators list contains only U3; U6 is missing entirely.

U2 and U4 are CPC1017N photoMOSFET optocouplers used as DC/AC coupling switches on the oscilloscope input signal path. They are driven by signals DC_SW and OP_SW. The isolation_barriers list is empty. These are classified as 'ic' but not identified as switching/isolation components.

Every named net in the nets section has pins: []. For example, I2C_SDA has point_count: 7 but pins: []. Only nets with explicit pins defined in the component symbol (like GND, ALT_VAA-, ALT_VAA+ via L2/L4 pins, and D1/D2 pins) have populated pin lists. This means no pin-to-net mapping is available for any IC or most passives, making voltage divider detection, RC filter detection, and bus protocol detection impossible. Only the 2 inductors (L2, L4) and D1, D2, P1, Y1 have populated pins because they have explicit pin definitions in the legacy library.
